title: Tomb of Meir - Pepyankh the Middle
description: Area: Meir (Mir). Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). Tomb of the nomarch Pepyankh, known as "Henkem". Features colorful daily life scenes.

title: Tomb of Meir - Senbi
description: Area: Meir (Mir). Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). Tomb of Senbi, son of Ukhhotep. Famous for desert hunting scenes.

title: Tomb of Meir - Ukh-Hotep
description: Area: Meir (Mir). Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). Tomb of Ukhhotep, son of Senbi. Contains depictions of the deceased fowling and fishing.

title: Tomb of Meir - Senbi II
description: Area: Meir (Mir). Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). Another tomb belonging to the Senbi family lineage of Cusae nomarchs.

title: Tomb of Meir - Ukh-Hotep II
description: Area: Meir (Mir). Era: Middle Kingdom. Tomb of Ukh-Hotep II, showcasing provincial art styles distinct from the capital.

title: Asyut Western Mountain - Stabl Antar
description: Area: Gebel Asyut al-Gharbi. Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). Great rock-cut tomb of Hapidjefa I, later used as a quarry and stable.

title: Tomb of Hapidjefa I
description: Area: Gebel Asyut al-Gharbi. Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). The largest private rock-cut tomb of the Middle Kingdom, belonging to the Great Overlord of the Lycopolite nome.

title: Tomb of Kheti I
description: Area: Gebel Asyut al-Gharbi. Era: First Intermediate Period / Middle Kingdom. Rock-cut tomb of the nomarch Kheti I (Tomb III).

title: Tomb of Itibi
description: Area: Gebel Asyut al-Gharbi. Era: First Intermediate Period / Middle Kingdom. Tomb of the nomarch Itibi (Tomb IV), featuring inscriptions on the Herakleopolitan civil war.

title: Tomb of Kheti II
description: Area: Gebel Asyut al-Gharbi. Era: First Intermediate Period / Middle Kingdom. Tomb of nomarch Kheti II, known for soldiers depicted on the walls (Tomb V).

title: Speos of Asyut (Historical)
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: New Kingdom. Historical reference to a Speos or temple dedicated to Wepwawet, likely near the modern city.

title: Deir Rifa - Tomb of the Two Brothers
description: Area: Deir Rifa. Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). Famous tomb of Nakht-Ankh and Khnum-Nakht, where the famous "Two Brothers" mummies were found.

title: Deir Rifa - Rock-Cut Tombs
description: Area: Deir Rifa. Era: Middle Kingdom to New Kingdom. General necropolis of the 7th Upper Egyptian Nome (Shas-Hotep), containing tombs of local officials.

title: Deir Rifa - Coptic Monastery
description: Area: Deir Rifa. Era: Coptic / Byzantine. The ancient rock tombs were reused by Coptic monks, forming a monastery complex dedicated to St. Mary.

title: El-Hemamieh (Hammamiya) - Tomb of Kakhent
description: Area: El-Hemamieh. Era: Old Kingdom (5th Dynasty). Rock-cut tomb of local official Kakhent.

title: El-Hemamieh (Hammamiya) - Badarian Site
description: Area: El-Hemamieh. Era: Predynastic (Badarian). Key archaeological site defining the Badarian culture, exploring early settlements and cemeteries.

title: Qaw el-Kebir - Tomb of Wahka I
description: Area: Qaw el-Kebir (Antaeopolis). Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). Monumental terraced tomb of the nomarch Wahka I.

title: Qaw el-Kebir - Tomb of Wahka II
description: Area: Qaw el-Kebir (Antaeopolis). Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). The grandest of the terraced tombs, belonging to Wahka II, with columned halls.

title: Qaw el-Kebir - Tomb of Ibu
description: Area: Qaw el-Kebir (Antaeopolis). Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty). Another significant rock-cut tomb in the Qaw necropolis.

title: Temple of Antaeopolis Remains
description: Area: Qaw el-Kebir. Era: Ptolemaic/Roman. Scanty remains of the temple dedicated to Antaeus (Nemty), largely destroyed by the Nile.

title: Deir el-Gabrawi - Tomb of Ibi
description: Area: Deir el-Gabrawi. Era: Old Kingdom (6th Dynasty). Important tomb of Ibi, Overlord of the Abydos Nome, with detailed wall reliefs.

title: Deir el-Gabrawi - Tomb of Djau
description: Area: Deir el-Gabrawi. Era: Old Kingdom (6th Dynasty). Tomb of Djau, son of Ibi, reflecting the power of provincial governors.

title: Deir el-Gabrawi - Northern Group
description: Area: Deir el-Gabrawi. Era: Old Kingdom. A cluster of tombs belonging to minor officials of the 12th Nome.

title: Shutb (Shashotep)
description: Area: Shutb. Era: Pharaonic / Islamic. The site of ancient Shashotep, capital of the 11th Nome. Contains mounds and reused blocks.

title: Arab el-Madabegh
description: Area: Manqabad/Asyut. Era: Pharaonic. Site yielding pottery and remains from various eras, adjacent to the limestone cliffs.

title: Alexan Pasha Palace
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: 1910 AD (Modern). A magnificent riverside palace blending European and Islamic styles, built for Alexan Pasha Abnoub.

title: Wikalat Shalabi
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: Ottoman Period. A historic caravanserai (wikala) used for trade, featuring traditional Islamic architecture.

title: Al-Mujahidin Mosque
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: Ottoman Period. One of the oldest mosques in Asyut, built on a high mound, known for its distinct minaret.

title: Al-Faramawy Mosque
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: Ottoman Period. A significant historical mosque located in the heart of old Asyut.

title: Al-Kashif Mosque
description: Area: Manfalout. Era: Mamluk / Ottoman. Historic mosque located in the city of Manfalout.

title: Asyut Barrage (Old)
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: 1903 AD (British Era). A historic dam built by Sir William Willcocks to regulate the Nile flow.

title: Al-Muharraq Monastery (Deir al-Muharraq)
description: Area: Al-Qusiya. Era: 4th Century AD. One of the most holy Christian sites, believed to be a resting place of the Holy Family (Mount Qusqam).

title: Church of the Virgin (Al-Muharraq)
description: Area: Al-Qusiya (inside Al-Muharraq). Era: 1st Century AD (Tradition). An ancient church considered one of the oldest altars in the world.

title: Keep (Al-Hosn) of Al-Muharraq
description: Area: Al-Qusiya (inside Al-Muharraq). Era: Medieval. A defensive tower used by monks during raids, well-preserved.

title: Deir Durunka (Virgin Mary Monastery)
description: Area: Durunka (Western Mountain). Era: 1st Century AD (Tradition). Important pilgrimage site where the Holy Family is believed to have stayed in a cave.

title: Deir El-Ganadla
description: Area: El-Ganadla. Era: 4th Century AD. Ancient Coptic monastery located in the desert, retaining old architectural features.

title: Deir al-Jawla (Deir al-Gabrawi nearby)
description: Area: East of Nile. Era: Coptic Era. Monastery located near the Deir al-Gabrawi cliffs, often associated with the rock tombs.

title: Hanging Monastery (Deir el-Muallaq)
description: Area: Abnub (Al-Maabda). Era: 4th Century AD. Built into the cliff face of Gebel Abnub, accessible by a steep path.

title: Deir Tasa
description: Area: Tasa / Badari. Era: Predynastic / Coptic. Site famous for the "Tasian Culture" (Predynastic) and a Coptic monastery.

title: Sidi Galal El-Suyuti Tomb
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: Mamluk/Ottoman. Mausoleum of the famous scholar Jalal al-Din al-Suyuti (though his main tomb is in Cairo, this is a local shrine).

title: Hammam Thabet
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: Ottoman Period. One of the few remaining historic public baths (hammams) in Asyut.

title: Abnub Necropolis
description: Area: Abnub. Era: Old Kingdom / First Intermediate. Cemetery site on the east bank containing tombs of provincial officials.

title: Al-Badari Archaeological Site
description: Area: Al-Badari. Era: Predynastic (4400–4000 BC). The type site for the Badarian culture, crucial for understanding Predynastic Egypt.

title: Kom Isfaht
description: Area: Isfaht. Era: Greco-Roman. An archaeological mound (tell) with remains of a temple dedicated to Apollo/Horus.

title: Zerayib Quarry
description: Area: Ronka / Durunka. Era: New Kingdom. Ancient limestone quarries used for building temples, containing graffito.

title: Tomb of Mesehti
description: Area: Asyut (Gebel Asyut). Era: Middle Kingdom. Tomb famous for the discovery of two sets of wooden soldier models (Egyptian and Nubian archers).

title: Shorta (Police) Club Museum
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: Modern. Small local museum housing artifacts found in the region (status and accessibility varies).

title: Deir Abu Maqar of Asyut
description: Area: Asyut. Era: Coptic. Historical monastery dedicated to St. Macarius.

title: Minaret of Zaghloul Mosque
description: Area: Asyut City. Era: Ottoman/Mamluk. Located in the old city, known for its historic architectural style.

title: Gebel el-Haridi (South Asyut Limit)
description: Area: Border of Asyut/Sohag. Era: Old/Middle Kingdom & Coptic. Spectacular cliffs with rock-cut hermitages and inscriptions.
